title: quercus × egglestonii
intro: Quercus × egglestonii, also known as the Eggleston oak or hybrid oak, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which are primarily attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Quercus × egglestonii include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
